chapter_title.en: Reward of inscribing Maashaa Allaah… on one’s ring

text: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُمَرَ یَرْفَعُهُ إِلَی أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ کَتَبَ عَلَی خَاتَمِهِ ما شاءَ اللَّهُ لا قُوَّهَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ أَمِنَ مِنَ الْفَقْرِ الْمُدْقِعِ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ahmad who said it was narrated to me by ’Amru b. Ali from his uncle Muhammad b. Umar who raised it to Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who inscribes: Maashaa Allaahu laa quwwata illaa billaahi astaghfirullaah on his ring, will remain safe from devastating poverty.”
